com.hp.workpath.api.access

Class UserOverridesAttributes.Builder

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.hp.workpath.api.access.UserOverridesAttributes.Builder
  • Enclosing class:
    UserOverridesAttributes

    public static class UserOverridesAttributes.Builder
extends java.lang.Object
    Builder for building user's override parameters.
    Since:
    API 2

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Builder()
      Default constructor a new Builder with default attributes.

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods

      Adds multiple "Bcc" values to an email message.

      Adds "Cc" value to an email message.

      Adds multiple "Cc" values to an email message.

      Adds "To" value to an email message.
      UserOverridesAttributes.Builder addToAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)

      Sets Subject for an email message.

    • Constructor Detail

      • Builder

        public Builder()
        Default constructor a new Builder with default attributes.
        Since:
        API 2

    • Method Detail

      • addToAddress

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder addToAddress(java.lang.String emailAddress,
                                                    java.lang.String name)
        Adds "To" value to an email message. Includes optional "Name" parameter for recipient's display name.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:
        emailAddress - email address of the recipient
        name - name of the recipient
        Returns:
        Builder builder with addToAddress.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if emailAddress is null
        Since:
        API 2

      • addToAddresses

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder addToAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)
        Adds multiple "To" values to an email message.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:
        emailAddresses - array of email addresses
        Returns:
        Builder builder with addToAddresses.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if emailAddresses is null
        Since:
        API 2

      • addCcAddress

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder addCcAddress(java.lang.String emailAddress,
                                                    java.lang.String name)
        Adds "Cc" value to an email message. Includes optional "Name" parameter for recipient's display name.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:
        emailAddress - email address of the recipient
        name - name of the recipient
        Returns:
        Builder builder with addCcAddress.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if emailAddress is null
        Since:
        API 2

      • addCcAddresses

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder addCcAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)
        Adds multiple "Cc" values to an email message.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:
        emailAddresses - array of email addresses
        Returns:
        Builder builder with addToAddresses.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if emailAddresses is null
        Since:
        API 2

      • addBccAddress

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder addBccAddress(java.lang.String emailAddress,
                                                     java.lang.String name)
        Adds "Bcc" value to an email message. Includes optional "Name" parameter for recipient's display name.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:
        emailAddress - email address of the recipient
        name - name of the recipient
        Returns:
        Builder builder with addBccAddress.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if emailAddress is null
        Since:
        API 2

      • addBccAddresses

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder addBccAddresses(java.lang.String... emailAddresses)
        Adds multiple "Bcc" values to an email message.

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:
        emailAddresses - array of email addresses
        Returns:
        Builder builder with addToAddresses.
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if emailAddresses is null
        Since:
        API 2

      • setFrom

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder setFrom(java.lang.String emailAddress,
                                               java.lang.String name)
        Sets "From" for an email message

        This is an e-mail address in the form described by RFC 5322, section 3.4 (mailbox address), except that such addresses may not contain new lines (but may contain comments). Note that this means that an address may take either of the following forms:

        • [display-name] <addr-spec>.
        Examples: John Doe <j.doe@acme.com>.

        Parameters:
        emailAddress - email address of the sender
        name - sender name
        Returns:
        this builder for method chaining
        Throws:
        java.lang.NullPointerException - if emailAddress or name is null
        Since:
        API 2

      • setSubject

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder setSubject(java.lang.String subject)
        Sets Subject for an email message.
        Parameters:
        subject - subject for email message
        Returns:
        this builder for method chaining
        Since:
        API 2

      • setMessage

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder setMessage(java.lang.String message)
        Sets text content for an email message.
        Parameters:
        message - email message body text
        Returns:
        this builder for method chaining
        Since:
        API 2

      • setFaxCompanyName

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder setFaxCompanyName(java.lang.String faxCompanyName)
        Sets fax company name (min length=1, max length=30, UTF-8, may be null).

        Will be used by the device to automatically populate the fax company name field, if the fax app allows the field to be edited. The setting will return to the guest user default when the user session ends. A null value indicates that the producer does not wish override the value, deferring to other sources. This value cannot be cleared.

        Parameters:
        faxCompanyName - fax company name
        Returns:
        this builder for method chaining
        Since:
        API 2

      • setFaxBillingCode

        public UserOverridesAttributes.Builder setFaxBillingCode(java.lang.String faxBillingCode)
        Sets fax billing code. (max length=16, UTF-8, may be null).

        The supplied value must conform to the device's minimum fax billing code length setting. Otherwise, if it is used the fax job will fail. May be used by the device to automatically populate the fax billing code field, if the fax app allows the field to be edited. The setting will return to the guest user default when the user session ends. A null value indicates that the producer does not wish override the value, deferring to other sources. A empty string effectively clears the value.

        Parameters:
        faxBillingCode - fax billing code
        Returns:
        this builder for method chaining
        Since:
        API 2

      • build

        public UserOverridesAttributes build()
                              throws CapabilitiesExceededException
        Combines all of the attributes in this into a UserOverridesAttributes object.
        Returns:
        User's overrides attributes containing all of the attributes.
        Throws:
        CapabilitiesExceededException - if attributes are out of supported range.
        Since:
        API 2
